skill.name: workflow-orchestration
skill.description: Coordinates CI/CD workflow steps across validation, review, commit-message generation, CI recovery, and PR delegation tools.
skill.summary: Orchestrates validation, review, CI recovery, and PR handoff workflows with evidence-based checkpoints.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command-like Markdown examples plus high-risk keyword matches. Review found a documentation-only DevOps orchestration skill with legitimate git, validation, CI, and plugin workflow instructions; no malicious intent, prompt injection, exfiltration, weak cryptography, C2 behavior, or reconnaissance was found. Because the skill instructs agents to run local scripts, git commands, and integration workflows, it should publish with an external-command warning.

medium_findings.title: External Command Workflow Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents through local validation scripts, git status and diff checks, CI monitoring scripts, plugin invocation, and PR delegation. These commands are consistent with the stated DevOps purpose and include human approval before commits, but they can affect a repository and should be treated as an operational risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly contain shell commands or execution steps for validation, git inspection, CI monitoring, and plugin workflows. The context is legitimate CI/CD orchestration rather than arbitrary or hidden command execution.

low_findings.title: False-Positive High-Risk Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The static C2, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance findings appear to be keyword collisions in normal prose or examples. The reviewed lines describe CI/CD workflow text, pending work, shellcheck diagnostics, and state reporting, with no cryptographic implementation, command-and-control logic, or network scanning behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found ordinary workflow documentation and no executable weak crypto, reconnaissance, or C2 code. The file is a Markdown guide, and no hidden scripts or network endpoints were present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository-Affecting Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes examples and workflow steps that run validation scripts, inspect git history and diffs, monitor CI, and update workflow state. These are expected for the skill purpose but require user trust in local repository scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command patterns are visible in Markdown and map directly to the skill purpose. Risk depends on the repository scripts and integrations that the agent executes at runtime.

content.value_statement: Multi-step delivery work often loses context between validation, review, commits, and CI recovery. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for chaining those steps with evidence checkpoints.
actual_capabilities: Selects a pre-commit, PR creation, or CI recovery pipeline based on the task.
Guides agents to gather context from CHANGELOG.md, git status, recent commits, and diffs.
Chains validation, code review, commit-message generation, and CI recovery steps.
Requires human approval before committing or delegating PR work.
Defines evidence-based completion summaries with step status and artifacts.
Provides retry and escalation rules for validation, review, CI, and Jules API failures.
limitations: Requires the referenced companion plugins or equivalent local workflows to be available.
Does not implement CI, review, commit, or PR APIs by itself.
Depends on local repository scripts, especially local validation scripts, being safe and correct.
Cannot guarantee CI recovery when failures need external credentials or human decisions.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Quality Gate
use_cases.description: Run validation, review changes, generate a commit message, and present evidence before a human approves the commit.

use_cases.title: CI Failure Recovery
use_cases.description: Use structured diagnosis, targeted fixes, retry limits, and validation evidence to recover from failing CI checks.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Handoff
use_cases.description: Coordinate final validation, human diff review, PR delegation, and status monitoring for completed feature work.

prompt_templates.title: Validate My Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-orchestration to run the pre-commit pipeline for this repository. Gather context, validate the changes, review risks, and summarize evidence before any commit.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-orchestration to inspect the pending changes, run validation, invoke review, draft a conventional commit message, and wait for my approval.

prompt_templates.title: Recover a Failed CI Run
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-orchestration for CI recovery. Diagnose the failing check, propose a focused fix, apply it if safe, rerun validation, and report retry history.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a PR Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-orchestration to complete the PR-create pipeline. Validate, review, present the diff for approval, delegate PR work if available, and monitor completion.

output_examples.input: Validate the current branch before I commit.
output: Context gathered from changelog, working tree status, and recent commits.
Validation and review results are summarized with any blocking issues and a recommended commit message.

output_examples.input: CI failed after my last push. Help recover it.
output: Failure evidence is grouped by root cause, proposed fix, validation result, and remaining risk.
The final report states whether recovery succeeded or needs human escalation.

output_examples.input: The feature is ready for a pull request.
output: The response includes validation status, review status, diff review checkpoint, delegation status, and PR handoff result.

best_practices: Run the workflow from a clean understanding of current git state and recent changelog entries.
Keep human approval before commits, PR delegation, or actions that publish changes.
Report concrete evidence for each pipeline step, including failures and skipped steps.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the orchestration plan as permission to commit without user approval.
Do not continue a pipeline after a failed validation step without reporting the failure.
Do not claim CI recovery is complete without rerunning the relevant local or remote checks.

faq.question: Does this skill run CI/CD by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides orchestration instructions and depends on local scripts, git, CI tooling, and companion skills.

faq.question: Can it create commits automatically?
faq.answer: The workflow requires human approval before commits. It can draft a commit message and present evidence first.

faq.question: Which tools does it coordinate?
faq.answer: It references validation tooling, git commands, code-review, smart-commit, autonomous-ci, and optional Jules integration.

faq.question: Is it useful without every companion plugin?
faq.answer: Partly. The structure still helps, but unavailable plugins must be replaced with equivalent manual or local steps.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to run local commands and scripts. Users should review repository scripts before executing them.

faq.question: What output should I expect?
faq.answer: Expect a pipeline summary with selected workflow, step results, evidence, blockers, and actions needing human approval.
